summary: Comprehensive E2E validation of Bookvia multi-branch (Sucursales) feature. Backend: 16/16 pytest cases PASS covering auto-migration, idempotency, booking backfill, CRUD (create/patch/delete), 422 validation, auth gating (401/403 for guest, 403 for non-business user), set-primary atomicity (exactly 1 is_primary per business), inactive branch promotion blocked (400), primary delete blocked (400), non-primary soft-delete (is_active=false, not removed), public endpoint (no auth, returns only is_active=true, primary first, 404 for unknown business). Frontend (testbiz_dashboard@test.com on /business/dashboard?tab=branches): branches-tab present, branch-create-btn present, 5 branch cards rendered with primary badge atomicity (exactly 1 primary), edit/set-primary/toggle/delete buttons present (delete hidden on primary -> 4 delete btns for 5 cards), modal opens correctly, empty-submit shows validation toast 'Completa nombre, dirección, ciudad y estado', valid submit creates branch (cards 5->6), edit pre-populates form fields and saves changes, set-primary atomically moves primary badge with only 1 primary on page after promotion. Regression OK: /api/status=200, payment-mode card 'Conectar Stripe' visible, navbar dropdown + notification bell visible, dashboard tabs (Agenda/Reportes/Clientes/Servicios/Equipo/Sucursales/Cierres/Fotos/Suscripcion Actividad) all present.
verified_features: GET /api/businesses/me/branches auto-creates primary branch on first call for legacy business (test_auto_migration_creates_primary).
Subsequent GETs do not duplicate primary; same id returned (test_auto_migration_idempotent).
Backfill verified via direct Motor query: 0 bookings remain without branch_id for testbiz_dashboard (test_backfill_bookings_have_branch_id).
POST /api/businesses/me/branches with valid body -> 200 + is_primary=false + is_active=true.
POST without auth -> 401/403; POST as regular user (test@example.com) -> 401/403/404 (require_business).
PATCH /api/businesses/me/branches/{id} updates fields and persists (verified via subsequent GET).
POST /set-primary demotes current primary and promotes target atomically -> exactly 1 primary remains.
DELETE non-primary branch without future bookings -> 200 with soft-delete (is_active=false, still exists).
GET /api/businesses/{biz_id}/branches public (no auth) -> 200, only is_active=true, primary first.
GET public branches for unknown business -> 404.
Frontend: branches-tab + create btn render; cards render with primary badge correctly; delete btn hidden on primary; modal validation + create + edit + set-primary flows all work end-to-end.
critical_code_review_comments: set-primary uses two sequential update_many+update_one calls — not transactionally atomic. A simultaneous request could theoretically result in 0 primaries during the window (~ms). For production scale consider a single $set in a Mongo transaction or replace with a 'primary_branch_id' pointer on the business document. Acceptable for current MVP traffic.

_serialize_branch services_count metric counts services with branch_id=null AND business_id match. That's a deliberate inclusive fallback for legacy single-branch services, but it means all branches of a business will show the same services_count for legacy services. Once services get branch_id properly assigned this resolves itself. Worth a code comment.

delete_branch checks upcoming bookings only by date>=today and status in (pending,confirmed). Doesn't check for active bookings on today AFTER current time would be missed if status moved to 'in_progress' — verify status enum coverage.

_ensure_primary_branch backfills bookings but NOT services. Services with branch_id=null will continue to behave as 'shared' (covered by the $or in metric query) — acceptable but worth documenting as the chosen behaviour in the docstring.

Manager (is_manager) is blocked from create/update/delete/set-primary correctly via 403 in router code. Tested implicitly via require_business + is_manager check. No manager-credentialed test in scope but logic is symmetric across all 4 endpoints — good.
Pydantic BranchCreate requires name/address/city/state (no Optional default) — produces clean 422 on missing fields. Good.
ObjectId _id excluded in _serialize_branch — good (uses out.pop('_id', None)).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on exercised flows (tab render, card render, primary badge atomicity, delete hide on primary, modal open/validate/create/edit/set-primary, regression checks)

test_credentials: Business: testbiz_dashboard@test.com / TestBiz123! used for both backend and frontend testing. Regular user: test@example.com / TestPass123! used for require_business negative test.
seed_data_creation: Created 4 test branches for testbiz_dashboard (TEST_Sucursal_XXX, TEST_Inactive_XXX, TEST_ToDelete_XXX, TEST_UI_XXX). One was soft-deleted (is_active=false). One was promoted to primary via UI test. Cleanup safe: db.branches.delete_many({'name': {'$regex': '^TEST_'}}) — but consider restoring 'Test Dashboard Business - Principal' as primary first (or just db.branches.update_many({'business_id':<id>},{'$set':{'is_primary':false}}) then update one named '... Principal' to is_primary=true). 1 primary on testbiz_dashboard at end of test run is named 'TEST_UI_104695'.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `set -a && . /app/frontend/.env && . /app/backend/.env && set +a && cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_branches_flow.py -v`. The current primary branch on testbiz_dashboard is the UI-created TEST_UI_xxx — if you re-run from a clean DB the auto-migration will recreate 'Test Dashboard Business - Principal' as primary. The set_primary code is not transactional (sequential updates) — flagged as code review comment, not a bug. Pre-existing event loop issue in test_decommission_flow.py (RuntimeError: no current event loop) is unrelated to branches and should be reported separately. ipapi.co CORS errors in console are pre-existing (iteration_101+).
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — all spec scenarios behave correctly. No defects found in branches feature.
